concept.name: justify-self
concept.explain:
Use justify-self to align an item inside its grid area on the horizontal axis.

Common values:
start → left
center → center
end → right

Example:
justify-self: end;

This moves the item to the right side of its grid area without changing its grid-column or grid-row.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in the same area and align it to the right edge using `justify-self`.
hints: Use justify-self to move the car horizontally inside its grid area.
The car should move to the right edge.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 24px; justify-self: end;

concept.name: align-self
concept.explain:
Use align-self to align an item inside its grid area on the vertical axis.

Common values:
start → top
center → middle
end → bottom

Example:
align-self: end;

This moves the item to the bottom of its grid area without changing its grid-column or grid-row.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in the same area and align it to the bottom edge using `align-self`.
hints: Use align-self to move the car vertically inside its grid area.
The car should move to the bottom edge.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-height: 24px; align-self: end;

concept.name: place-self
concept.explain:
Use place-self as a shorthand for align-self and justify-self.

Common values:
start → top left
center → center
end → bottom right

Example:
place-self: center;

This centers the item inside its grid area on both axes.

ui.prompt: Goal: Keep the car in the same area and center it inside the parking bay using `place-self`.
hints: Use place-self to align the car on both axes at once.
The car should sit in the center of its grid area.

entities.baseCss: background: rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.22); outline: 2px dashed rgba(200, 0, 0, 0.8); border-radius: 10px; min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; place-self: center;
